company_profile.name: LendingTree, Inc.
company_profile.type_rationale: Financial services — DNI and P/B prioritised
company_profile.description: LendingTree, Inc., through its subsidiary, operates online consumer platform in the United States. The company operates through three segments: Home, Consumer, and Insurance. The Home segments offer purchase mortgage, refinance mortgage, and home equity loans and lines of credit. The Consumer segment provides credit cards; personal, small business, and auto loans; deposit accounts; and other credit products, such as debt settlement services. The Insurance segment includes information, tools, and access to insurance quote products, including automobile, home, life, and health and Medicare through which consumers are matched with insurance lead aggregators to obtain insurance offers and policies. This segment also offers QuoteWizard, a marketplace for insurance comparison; and ValuePenguin, a personal finance website that offers consumers objective analysis on various financial topics. The company was formerly known as Tree.com, Inc. and changed its name to LendingTree, Inc. in January 2015. LendingTree, Inc. was incorporated in 1996 and is based in Charlotte, North Carolina.
company_profile.description_summary:
Operates an online marketplace connecting consumers with lenders and insurance providers across the United States. Generates revenue by matching borrowers with mortgage, auto, and personal loan providers, as well as credit card and insurance companies through its three business segments: Home, Consumer, and Insurance.

The company distinguishes itself through specialized financial comparison tools, including QuoteWizard for insurance shopping and ValuePenguin for personal finance analysis, enabling consumers to obtain competitive quotes across multiple products and providers.

_meta.type_rationale: Financial services — DNI and P/B prioritised
_meta.method_explanation: Consumer finance companies — such as credit card providers or personal lenders — earn income from the interest they charge on loans. Like banks, they carry a lot of debt by design because lending money is their core business model. We use net income DCF and Price-to-Book, which together capture both the earning power and the asset value of the loan book.

_notes.fcf_vs_debt: Free cash flow is the cash left over after the company has paid for everything it needs to run and grow the business. This chart compares that cash to the company's total debt. A company with rising free cash flow and falling debt is in a strong financial position. If debt is rising faster than cash, the company may be taking on more risk than it can comfortably manage.
_notes.shares_vs_buybacks: When a company buys back its own shares, it reduces the total number of shares in circulation — which means each remaining share represents a larger slice of the business. This chart shows how the share count has changed over time. Consistent buybacks are generally a sign that management believes the stock is undervalued and wants to return cash to shareholders.
_notes.returns_trajectory: Return on Equity (ROE) measures how much profit a company generates for every dollar of shareholder money invested. Return on Invested Capital (ROIC) is similar but also includes debt. Both are expressed as a percentage — higher is better. A company consistently earning above 15% ROIC is generally considered to have a genuine competitive advantage over its peers.
_notes.revenue_vs_ar: Accounts receivable is money the company is owed by customers who have not yet paid their bills. This chart compares revenue growth to accounts receivable growth each year. If accounts receivable is growing much faster than revenue, it may mean the company is struggling to collect payments — sometimes a sign of aggressive or fictitious revenue reporting. A healthy company should see both lines move at a similar pace.

company_profile.name: Thomson Reuters Corporation
company_profile.type_rationale: FCF compounder — FCF margin 21.9%; DCF methods prioritised
company_profile.description: Thomson Reuters Corporation operates as a content and technology company in the Americas, Europe, the Middle East, Africa, and the Asia Pacific. It operates through five segments: Legal Professionals, Corporates, Tax, Audit & Accounting Professionals, Reuters News, and Global Print. The Legal Professionals segment offers research and workflow products focusing on legal research and integrated legal workflow solutions that combine content, tools, and analytics to law firms and governments. The Corporates segment provides a suite of content-driven technologies, including generative AI and integrated compliance workflow solutions to small businesses and multinational organizations. Tax, Audit & Accounting Professionals segment offers research and workflow products to tax, audit, and accounting firms. The Reuters News segment provides business, financial, and international news to media organizations, professional, and news consumers through Reuters News Agency, Reuters.com, Reuters Events, Thomson Reuters products, and to financial firms. The Global Print segment offers legal and tax information primarily in print format. The company was formerly known as The Thomson Corporation and changed its name to Thomson Reuters Corporation in April 2008. The company was founded in 1799 and is headquartered in Toronto, Canada. Thomson Reuters Corporation is a subsidiary of The Woodbridge Company Limited.
company_profile.description_summary:
Thomson Reuters operates as a content and technology company serving legal, corporate, tax, and accounting professionals across multiple regions. It generates revenue primarily through legal research and workflow solutions, corporate compliance technologies, tax and accounting products, news distribution, and print publications.

The company distinguishes itself through its integration of generative AI into professional workflows and its global Reuters News platform, delivering business and financial news to media organizations and financial firms worldwide.

_meta.type_rationale: FCF compounder — FCF margin 21.9%; DCF methods prioritised
_meta.method_explanation: Free cash flow compounders are businesses that consistently generate more cash than they need to run the business — think of companies like consumer goods brands or software firms with subscription revenue. Because their cash flows are reliable year after year, a Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) model works very well. DCF asks: if I add up all the future cash flows this company will generate and adjust for the time value of money, what is it worth today? For these companies, that calculation gives a meaningful and trustworthy answer.

company_profile.name: Trustmark Corporation
company_profile.type_rationale: Bank or thrift — P/Tangible Book prioritised; DCF excluded
company_profile.description: Trustmark Corporation operates as the bank holding company for Trustmark National Bank that provides banking and other financial solutions to individuals and corporate institutions in the United States. It operates through General Banking and Wealth Management segments. The company offers checking, savings and money market accounts, and certificates of deposit, as well as certificates of deposit and individual retirement accounts; and treasury management services. It also provides loans comprising financing for commercial and industrial projects, income producing commercial real estate, owner-occupied real estate, and construction and land development; and installment and real estate loans and lines of credit. In addition, the company offers mortgage banking services, including construction financing, production of conventional and government-insured mortgages, and secondary marketing and mortgage servicing; wealth management and trust services, such as administration of personal trusts and estates; management of investment accounts for individuals, employee benefit plans, and charitable foundations; and institutional custody for large governmental entities and foundations, financial and estate planning, and retirement plan services. Further, it provides an intermediary vehicle for the provision of loans or investments in low-income communities. Trustmark Corporation was founded in 1889 and is headquartered in Jackson, Mississippi.
company_profile.description_summary: Trustmark operates as a bank holding company providing comprehensive financial services to individuals and corporations across the United States. Its primary revenue derives from General Banking, offering deposit accounts, loans for commercial and real estate projects, and mortgage banking services, alongside a Wealth Management segment providing trust administration, investment management, and financial planning services.

_meta.type_rationale: Bank or thrift — P/Tangible Book prioritised; DCF excluded
_meta.method_explanation: Banks make money from the difference between what they charge on loans and what they pay on deposits — not from traditional business cash flows. Because of this, standard cash flow models (DCF) do not work well for banks. Instead, we focus on Price-to-Book (P/B), which compares the stock price to the value of the bank's assets on its books. Banks also carry a lot of debt by design — they borrow money to lend it out — so high leverage is normal and not a red flag.

company_profile.name: Transcat, Inc.
company_profile.type_rationale: Standard company — balanced weighting across all methods
company_profile.description: Transcat, Inc. provides calibration and laboratory instrument services in the United States, Canada, and internationally. It operates through two segments, Service and Distribution. The Service segment offers calibration, repair, inspection, analytical qualification, preventative maintenance, consulting, and other related services. This segment also provides CalTrak, a proprietary document and asset management system that is used to manage the workflow of its calibration service centers and customers' assets; and Compliance, Control, and Cost, an online customer portal that provides its customers with web-based asset management capability, as well as a safe and secure off-site archive of calibration and other service records. The Distribution segment sells and rents test, measurement, and control instruments for customers' test and measurement instrumentation needs, as well as value-added services, such as calibration/certification of equipment purchase, equipment rental, used equipment for sale, and equipment kitting. This segment markets and sells its products through website, digital and print advertising, proactive outbound sales, and an inbound call center. The company provides services and products to highly regulated industries, principally life science, which includes companies in the pharmaceutical, biotechnology, medical device, and other FDA-regulated industries; and additional industries, including aerospace and defense, industrial manufacturing, energy and utilities, chemical manufacturing, and other industries that require accuracy in processes and confirmation of the capabilities of their equipment. Transcat, Inc. was incorporated in 1964 and is headquartered in Rochester, New York.

This company provides calibration and laboratory instrument services across North America and internationally, operating through two main divisions. company_profile.name: Trimble Inc.
company_profile.type_rationale: Standard company — balanced weighting across all methods
company_profile.description: Trimble Inc. offers technology solutions and platform that enable office professionals and field workers to connect workflows and industry lifecycles in North America, Europe, the Asia Pacific, and internationally. The company provides architecture and interior design; building information modeling, engineering, and virtual design and construction; and construction and owner software products. It also offers field and office software for estimating and job cost management, and project design and visualization; software for 3D design and data sharing; systems to guide and control construction equipment, such as excavators, bulldozers, wheel loaders, motor graders, and paving equipment; systems to monitor, track, and manage assets, equipment, and workers; and software to facilitate the management of the construction process and for sharing and communication of data in real time. In addition, it provides positioning services comprising VRSNow; CenterPoint RTX; FieldPoint RTX; Rangepoint RTX; ViewPoint RTX; and Trimble xFill services. Further, it offers transportation and logistics solutions for shippers, carriers, retailers, and intermediaries, such as carrier transportation management software (TMS) and maintenance solutions to manage core transportation operations and maintenance workflows; transporeon solutions to provide a cloud-based ecosystem that manages the transportation lifecycle from freight sourcing and procurement through transport execution, dock and yard management, and auditing; and MAPS, a mapping and routing solutions that provide the industry standard for truck-specific routing, mileage, and navigation. It sells its technology solutions directly to end users and through software integrations. The company was formerly known as Trimble Navigation Limited and changed its name to Trimble Inc. in October 2016. Trimble Inc. was founded in 1978 and is headquartered in Westminster, Colorado.
company_profile.description_summary:
Trimble provides software and technology solutions connecting workflows across construction, design, and transportation industries globally. Its offerings span architecture and building information modeling, construction equipment guidance systems, asset tracking, and positioning services. The company also delivers transportation management software and logistics solutions for carriers and shippers, selling directly to end users and through software integrations.

Trimble's distinctive strength lies in its integrated platform approach, linking office professionals and field workers across entire industry lifecycles while offering specialized solutions from equipment automation to real-time data communication and cloud-based ecosystem management.

quality_narrative.moat_rationale: Trimble's modest profitability and weak returns on capital suggest limited competitive advantage, despite adequate margins and predictable revenue; the business lacks the pricing power or structural defensibility needed for a durable moat.

metric_assessments.profitability: A 12.4% profit margin is solid and sits comfortably above the 8–10% range typical of industrial software and hardware companies, which is a genuine strength. The company is converting sales into profit at a respectable clip, showing operational discipline. However, the underlying 8.25% return on equity is concerning—that's roughly half what you'd expect from a healthy business—meaning Trimble isn't generating strong returns from shareholder money, which caps the profitability score well below excellent.
metric_assessments.financial_strength: The current ratio of 1.09 is just barely adequate, sitting at the low end of the safe zone (1.0–1.5 is typical for stable companies), and the debt-to-EBITDA ratio of 1.63 is moderate but not alarming for a technology company. Trimble can cover its short-term bills, but there's little cushion for unexpected trouble. The combination of thin liquidity and meaningful leverage means the company has less financial flexibility than investors might want, which is why this score remains middle-of-the-road rather than reassuring.
metric_assessments.growth: Revenue growth of 11.8% is respectable on its surface, but the projected 15.1% earnings-per-share growth over five years is only slightly higher, signaling that earnings aren't accelerating faster than sales—a sign of margin pressure or reinvestment struggles. More importantly, the data flags that growth is constrained, meaning structural or market limitations are capping the company's expansion potential. This is the weakest link in Trimble's profile and explains why the stock carries a 'Dangerous' classification despite adequate profitability.
metric_assessments.predictability: An 'Adequate' predictability score reflects stable margins and consistent revenue trends, which suggest a reasonably reliable business model that investors can forecast with moderate confidence. Revenue growth of 11.8% is neither erratic nor exceptional, pointing to a company with established market position but limited volatility in its earnings. The main weakness is that constrained growth limits visibility into future expansion, making it harder for analysts to project multi-year trajectories with real conviction.
metric_assessments.valuation: A 5/10 valuation score indicates the stock is neither cheap nor expensive—it's fairly priced relative to its fundamentals. With modest profitability, weak returns on capital, and constrained growth, there's little reason to expect the market to award Trimble a premium multiple. An investor isn't getting a bargain, but you're also not overpaying for hype; the risk-reward is neutral, which is why the valuation matches the mediocre business quality.

quality_narrative.classification_reason: Trimble earned a 'Dangerous' rating because it combines constrained growth, weak capital efficiency, and tight liquidity in a way that creates meaningful downside risk. The company's 8.25% return on equity and 6.67% return on invested capital are well below what strong businesses generate, yet it carries a debt-to-EBITDA of 1.63 and minimal cash cushion—leaving little room for error if market conditions soften. For a beginner, this means: the business is profitable and stable today, but if growth stalls or margins compress, the debt burden becomes harder to manage, and the stock could face pressure.
quality_narrative.investment_narrative: Trimble is a profitable, reasonably stable technology company with solid 12.4% margins and consistent 11.8% revenue growth, suggesting a legitimate operating business rather than a turnaround or speculation. However, it suffers from weak returns on capital (8% ROE, 6.7% ROIC), meaning the company is not generating attractive profits relative to the money invested in it, and growth is explicitly constrained by market or structural limits—both red flags for long-term value creation. The balance sheet is adequate but tight, with a current ratio near 1.0 and meaningful debt, leaving little safety margin if business momentum falters. The stock is fairly valued at current levels given these middling fundamentals, but there's no margin of safety to offset the growth headwinds and capital efficiency challenges. For most beginners, Trimble is a hold-and-watch, not a buy—the risk profile doesn't justify the modest returns on offer.

_meta.type_rationale: Bank or thrift — P/Tangible Book prioritised; DCF excluded
_meta.method_explanation: Banks make money from the difference between what they charge on loans and what they pay on deposits — not from traditional business cash flows. Because of this, standard cash flow models (DCF) do not work well for banks. Instead, we focus on Price-to-Book (P/B), which compares the stock price to the value of the bank's assets on its books. Banks also carry a lot of debt by design — they borrow money to lend it out — so high leverage is normal and not a red flag.

company_profile.description: Tower Semiconductor Ltd., an independent semiconductor foundry, provides technology, development, and process platforms for integrated circuits in the United States, Japan, rest of Asia, and Europe. The company provides customizable process technologies, including SiGe, SiPho, mixed signal CMOS, RF CMOS, CMOS image sensor, non-imaging sensors, wafers, and integrated power management. It also offers a design enablement platform for a design cycle, as well as transfer optimization and development process services to integrated device manufacturers and fabless companies. The company serves consumer applications, personal computers, communications, handsets and smartphones, automotive, industrial, aerospace, and medical devices. Tower Semiconductor Ltd. was incorporated in 1993 and is headquartered in Migdal Haemek, Israel
company_profile.description_summary:
An independent semiconductor foundry, this company provides customizable process technologies and platforms for integrated circuits globally. Its offerings include SiGe, mixed signal CMOS, RF CMOS, and CMOS image sensors, alongside design enablement services for integrated device manufacturers and fabless companies serving consumer, automotive, industrial, aerospace, and medical sectors.

The company distinguishes itself through specialized expertise in analog and mixed-signal technologies, offering tailored solutions that complement standard digital foundry services across diverse industrial applications.

_meta.type_rationale: Cyclical business (technology) — EV/EBITDA and P/S prioritised over P/E
_meta.method_explanation: Cyclical companies — such as semiconductors, mining, or manufacturing — have profits that rise sharply in good times and fall sharply in downturns. This makes earnings-based methods like P/E unreliable, because the earnings figure at any single point in time can be unusually high or low. EV/EBITDA smooths out some of this distortion by looking at earnings before interest and tax, making it a fairer comparison across the economic cycle. Price-to-Sales is also useful as revenue tends to be more stable than profits for cyclical businesses.

company_profile.name: Tetra Tech, Inc.
company_profile.type_rationale: Standard company — balanced weighting across all methods
company_profile.description: Tetra Tech, Inc. provides consulting and engineering services focusing on water, environment, and sustainable infrastructure in the United States and internationally. The company operates through two segments, Government Services Group (GSG) and Commercial/International Services Group (CIG). The GSG segment offers consulting and engineering services, including water resources analysis and water management, environmental monitoring, data analytics, government consulting, waste management, and civil infrastructure master planning and resilient engineering design for facilities, as well as transportation and local development projects primarily to government clients, including federal, state, and local, as well as international development agencies. It also offers sustainable solutions, such as energy management consulting, and greenhouse gas inventory assessment, certification, reduction, and management services. The CIG segment provides consulting and engineering services, including natural resources, energy, and utilities, as well as sustainable infrastructure master planning and engineering design for facilities; and transportation and local development projects to commercial and international clients, including the commercial and government sectors. The company offers early data collection and monitoring, data analysis and information management, science and engineering applied research, engineering design, project management, and operations and maintenance services; climate change consulting; greenhouse gas inventory assessment, certification, reduction, and management services; environmental remediation and reconstruction services, industrial water treatment and reuse services; and engineering services, such as data centers, advanced manufacturing, security systems, training and audiovisual facilities, clean rooms, laboratories, medical facilities, and disaster preparedness facilities. The company was founded in 1966 and is headquartered in Pasadena, California.
company_profile.description_summary:
Tetra Tech provides consulting and engineering services across water, environment, and sustainable infrastructure sectors globally. Operating through government and commercial segments, it generates revenue from federal, state, and local agencies plus private clients, offering services including water management, environmental monitoring, energy consulting, and infrastructure design.

The company distinguishes itself through comprehensive service offerings spanning data collection and analysis, climate change consulting, industrial water treatment, and specialized facility engineering for sectors like data centers and healthcare.

company_profile.type_rationale: Standard company — balanced weighting across all methods
company_profile.description: Turbo Energy, S.A. designs, develops, and distributes equipment for the generation, management, and storage of photovoltaic energy in Spain, rest of Europe, and internationally. The company offers lithium-ion batteries; inverters; photovoltaic modules; Go Solar, a portable photovoltaic product; and Sunbox, an AI based software system that monitors the generation, use, and management of photovoltaic energy. It is also involved in the acquisition, distribution, and sale of electrical and electronic materials for the development of renewable energy projects, such as solar panels, inverters, chargers, regulators, batteries, and structures. The company sells its products to installers and distributors for residential consumers. The company has a strategic partnership with Xiamen HiTHIUM Energy Storage Technology Co. Ltd. for the deployment of software-defined energy storage infrastructure across commercial and industrial applications in Europe and Latin America. The company was incorporated in 2013 and is based in Valencia, Spain. Turbo Energy, S.A. operates as a subsidiary of Umbrella Global Energy, S.A.
company_profile.description_summary:
This company designs, develops, and distributes photovoltaic energy equipment including lithium-ion batteries, inverters, and solar modules. It generates revenue through direct sales and distribution of renewable energy materials to installers and residential consumers across Spain, Europe, and internationally.

What distinguishes the company is its proprietary Sunbox AI software platform that monitors photovoltaic energy generation and management, plus a strategic partnership with a leading energy storage firm for deploying software-defined infrastructure across European and Latin American commercial sectors.

company_profile.name: TTM Technologies, Inc.
company_profile.type_rationale: Standard company — balanced weighting across all methods
company_profile.description: TTM Technologies, Inc. manufactures and sells mission systems, radio frequency (RF) components, RF microwave/microelectronic assemblies, and printed circuit boards (PCBs) and substrates in the United States, Taiwan, and internationally. It operates in three segments, A&D, Commercial, and RF&S Components. The company offers various engineered systems and RF and microwave assemblies; PCBs and IC substrates, custom assemblies and integrated systems, passive and advanced ceramic RF components, hi-reliability multi-chip modules, and beamforming and switching networks; and design-for-manufacturability, PCB layout design, simulation and testing, quick turnaround (QTA) production, and specialized RF assembly and testing. It also provides multi-mode surveillance and weather avoidance radar systems for fixed- and rotary-wing aircraft, Unmanned Aerial Vehicles, and shipboard platforms; AN/APS-153 multi-mode radar; communications suite within the MH-60R/S multi-mission helicopters; and supports maritime surveillance, counter UAS, and advanced air mobility end markets under MOSAIC brand. In addition, the company offers identification friend; monopulse secondary surveillance radars, and air traffic control systems; wired and wireless communication systems; designs, produces, and tests specialized multi-chip RF modules and components; and manufactures off-the-shelf surface mount microwave components. Further, it provides thick-film ceramic substrates, custom designed application specific integrated circuits, and conventional PCBs; custom electronic assemblies; QTA services; and thermal management. The company serves original equipment manufacturers, electronic manufacturing services providers, original design manufacturers, distributors, and government agencies; and aerospace and defense, data center computing, automotive, medical, industrial, instrumentation, and networking markets. TTM Technologies, Inc. was incorporated in 1978 and is headquartered in Santa Ana, California.
company_profile.description_summary:
Manufactures mission systems, RF components, microwave assemblies, and printed circuit boards for aerospace, defense, and commercial markets. Operates three segments: Aerospace & Defense, Commercial, and RF & Switching Components, serving original equipment manufacturers, government agencies, and various industries including data centers and automotive.

Distinctive capabilities include specialized radar systems for aircraft and maritime platforms, advanced ceramic RF components, quick-turnaround production services, and thermal management solutions, positioning it as a specialized supplier of complex engineered systems and high-reliability components.

_meta.type_rationale: FCF compounder — FCF margin 19.2%; DCF methods prioritised
_meta.method_explanation: Free cash flow compounders are businesses that consistently generate more cash than they need to run the business — think of companies like consumer goods brands or software firms with subscription revenue. Because their cash flows are reliable year after year, a Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) model works very well. DCF asks: if I add up all the future cash flows this company will generate and adjust for the time value of money, what is it worth today? For these companies, that calculation gives a meaningful and trustworthy answer.

company_profile.name: TaoWeave, Inc.
company_profile.type_rationale: Asset manager/broker — DNI prioritised; standard CF metrics unreliable
company_profile.description: TaoWeave, Inc. is a digital asset treasury company. The company focuses on Bittensor (TAO), a decentralized, open-source protocol that creates a marketplace for machine intelligence. It also enables AI models to be trained, shared, and monetized in a permissionless environment. In addition, it provides TAO a native token of the Bittensor network, used for staking, governance, and compensating AI model contributors. The company was formerly known as Oblong, Inc. and changed its name to TaoWeave, Inc. in December 2025. TaoWeave, Inc. is headquartered in Denver, Colorado.
company_profile.description_summary:
This digital asset treasury company focuses on Bittensor (TAO), a decentralized protocol that creates a marketplace for machine intelligence. It enables AI models to be trained, shared, and monetized permissionlessly while managing TAO, the network's native token used for staking, governance, and compensating contributors.

The company operates within a distinctive decentralized ecosystem that removes traditional barriers to AI model development and distribution, allowing direct economic participation for AI contributors through token-based incentives.

_meta.type_rationale: Asset manager/broker — DNI prioritised; standard CF metrics unreliable
_meta.method_explanation: Asset managers and brokers earn fees for managing other people's money. Their balance sheets hold client assets that do not belong to the firm, which makes standard balance sheet metrics like Price-to-Book misleading. Instead, we focus on net income and revenue-based methods, which better reflect the underlying fee-earning power of the business.

company_profile.name: Take-Two Interactive Software, Inc.
company_profile.type_rationale: Standard company — balanced weighting across all methods
company_profile.description: Take-Two Interactive Software, Inc. develops, publishes, and markets interactive entertainment solutions for consumers worldwide. The company develops and publishes action/adventure products under the Grand Theft Auto, LA Noire, Max Payne, Midnight Club, and Red Dead Redemption names, as well as other franchises. It also publishes various entertainment properties across various platforms and a range of genres, such as shooter, action, role-playing, strategy, sports, and family/casual entertainment under the BioShock, Mafia, Sid Meier's Civilization, XCOM series, Borderlands, and Tiny Tina's Wonderland names. In addition, the company publishes sports simulation titles comprising NBA 2K series, a basketball video game; the WWE 2K professional wrestling series; mobile titles, including WWE SuperCard; and PGA TOUR 2K. Further, it offers free-to-play mobile games, such as CSR Racing, Dragon City, Empires & Puzzles, FarmVille 3, Game of Thrones: Legends, Golf Rival, Harry Potter: Puzzles & Spells, Hit It Rich!, Match Factory!, Merge Dragons!, Merge Magic!, Monster Legends, NBA 2K, Toon Blast, Top Eleven, Wizard Of Oz Slots Casino, Toy Blast, Words With Friends, and Zynga Poker; and Color Block Jam. The company's products are designed for console gaming systems; and mobiles, such as smartphones, tablets, and personal computers. It provides its products through physical retail, digital download, online platforms, and cloud streaming services. Take-Two Interactive Software, Inc. was incorporated in 1993 and is based in New York, New York.
company_profile.description_summary:
This company develops, publishes, and markets interactive entertainment across multiple platforms and genres. It generates revenue from console games like Grand Theft Auto and Red Dead Redemption, sports simulations including NBA 2K and WWE 2K, and franchises such as BioShock, Borderlands, and Civilization, distributed through retail and digital channels.

The company distinctively operates a substantial free-to-play mobile gaming portfolio, featuring popular titles like Words With Friends, FarmVille 3, and Empires & Puzzles, complementing its premium console offerings.

_meta.type_rationale: Standard company — balanced weighting across all methods
_meta.method_explanation: This company does not fit neatly into a specialist category, so we apply a balanced mix of all ten valuation methods. These include Discounted Cash Flow (which estimates the present value of future cash flows), earnings multiples (P/E and PEG), revenue multiples (P/S and PSG), book value (P/B), and EV/EBITDA. Each method approaches value from a different angle — using all of them together gives a more rounded estimate than relying on any single number.

quality.note: Moat scored by Haiku API — final_score_pct computed out of 50.

quality_narrative.moat_rationale: Take-Two has a moderate moat built on recognizable game franchises and intellectual property, but profitability challenges, negative returns on equity, and margin pressure significantly weaken its competitive defensibility.

metric_assessments.profitability: Take-Two is unprofitable right now, with a negative 4.5% profit margin—meaning the company loses money on each dollar of sales rather than keeping it as profit. For context, healthy software and entertainment companies typically post margins of 15–25%, so this is a significant red flag. The underlying issue is that production costs for major game titles are enormous and not yet being offset by revenues; however, the company's scale and established franchises provide a foundation to eventually turn this around.
metric_assessments.financial_strength: With a current ratio of 1.24 and debt-to-EBITDA of 3.75x, Take-Two has adequate but not robust financial cushion. A current ratio above 1.2 means the company can cover its short-term obligations, which is acceptable, but debt-to-EBITDA of 3.75x is on the heavy side—healthy companies typically sit at 2–3x—indicating the business carries more leverage than is ideal given its unprofitability. The company is not in immediate danger, but there is limited room for error if revenues disappoint or costs rise further.
metric_assessments.growth: Revenue is growing at a modest 6.1% annually, which is adequate but uninspiring for a software and interactive entertainment firm, especially one carrying significant debt. Management guidance projects 37.8% earnings-per-share growth over the next five years, suggesting confidence in a turnaround to profitability; however, the company notes that growth is constrained, meaning expansion is hitting structural limits in the market. This disconnect between modest top-line growth and ambitious profit growth creates execution risk—the turnaround must happen through margin improvement rather than volume, which is harder to achieve.
metric_assessments.predictability: Take-Two's 6/10 predictability score reflects a business where franchise cycles and the timing of major game releases create uneven quarterly results, but the core fan bases for franchises like Grand Theft Auto and NBA 2K provide a stable baseline of demand. The company has proven it can generate recurring revenue through online multiplayer and in-game purchases, which is more predictable than one-off game sales alone. However, the long development cycles and infrequent major releases mean year-to-year revenue can swing significantly, preventing a higher score.
metric_assessments.valuation: At a 3/10 valuation score, Take-Two appears expensive relative to its current earnings power, not cheap. With a negative profit margin and negative return on equity of –10.56%, traditional valuation metrics (like price-to-earnings) are unhelpful or inverted, forcing investors to rely on price-to-sales or forward assumptions. The market is pricing in a material recovery in profitability; if that recovery does not materialize on schedule, the stock has limited margin of safety and significant downside risk.

quality_narrative.classification_reason: Take-Two earned a 'Dangerous' rating because it combines weak current profitability (negative margins and negative ROE) with material leverage (3.75x debt-to-EBITDA), leaving little margin for error. The company is betting on a turnaround that has not yet arrived, and if revenues slow or costs remain elevated, the debt burden becomes a serious problem. For a beginner, this means the stock requires patience and carries execution risk—it is a recovery story, not a stable business you can buy and hold without watching closely.
quality_narrative.investment_narrative: Take-Two is a legacy entertainment software company with valuable intellectual property in gaming franchises, but it is currently unprofitable and carrying substantial debt while growth slows—a risky combination. The business has strong brand recognition and a history of generating hit titles, and management believes it can return to healthy margins within five years; however, negative profit margins and negative returns on equity today show the company is burning through capital rather than creating it. Revenue growth of only 6% is modest for the industry, and the constraint on growth means profits must come from cutting costs or raising prices, both of which are harder to execute than growing your way to profitability. The stock is not cheap relative to where it needs to be, with the market pricing in a smooth recovery that may not arrive on schedule. For most beginner investors, this is a stock to watch but not to buy until the company returns to profitability and reduces its debt load.
quality_narrative.quality_conclusion: Take-Two Interactive Software, Inc. demonstrates weak fundamental quality and is constrained by profitability.
quality_narrative.risk_profile: Key risks to watch: elevated leverage (Debt/EBITDA 3.8x); balance sheet constraints on growth.
quality_narrative.investment_summary: With 37.8% consensus EPS growth forecast, the growth outlook supports the investment case. The stock trades near estimated intrinsic value and is warrants caution — careful position sizing and ongoing monitoring are essential.
